1	---
2	name: reference-retriever
3	description: Download the ORIGINAL source files (PDF + TeX/LaTeX when available) for papers, books, or online mathematical content (arXiv, journal preprints, online textbooks, Stacks Project, nLab) into references/, and register each with a lightweight pointer + table-of-contents stub. Dispatchable by the plan agent and by blueprint-writers when mathematical content needs a source not yet in references/.
4	write_domain: "references/**"
5	read_only: false
6	can_spawn: false
7	default_enabled: false
8	dispatcher_notes: |
9	  - Dispatch me whenever a strategic decision or a chapter you are
10	    about to write needs material not already in references/. Calling
11	    me is far cheaper than letting a writer or planner hallucinate
12	    sources or paper a hole with vague prose.
13	
14	  - I fetch the ORIGINAL source FILE (PDF, and TeX/LaTeX source when
15	    it is openly available), not a paraphrase. The companion .md I
16	    write next to it is only a citation pointer + table of contents,
17	    NOT a summary you can quote from. When you cite, open the actual
18	    downloaded source file (e.g. references/<slug>.pdf) and quote it
19	    verbatim — that is the whole point of having me fetch it.
20	
21	  - Plan agent: dispatch me BEFORE writing STRATEGY.md or composing
22	    blueprint-writer directives whenever the iteration touches a
23	    topic not represented in references/summary.md. Treat me as
24	    cheap source preparation, not a last-resort lookup.
25	
26	  - Blueprint-writer: when drafting reveals you need a source you
27	    don't have, dispatch me mid-session and wait for me to return
28	    before writing the affected chapter section. Note that to spawn
29	    me from inside a writer round, the planner must have declared
30	    `references/**` in your --write-domain at writer dispatch time
31	    (see blueprint-writer dispatcher_notes).
32	
33	  - I am NEVER dispatched by read-only subagents (blueprint-reviewer,
34	    lean-auditor, lean-vs-blueprint-checker). Those report what they
35	    see; they do not procure new sources.
36	
37	  - I do NOT fabricate. If the original file genuinely cannot be
38	    located through any legitimate open channel, I report "not found"
39	    rather than guessing content or writing a paraphrase in its place.
40	    Treat my "not found" as authoritative — do not redispatch with
41	    more aggressive prompting; instead reconsider the strategy.
42	
43	  - I download the source under references/<slug>.{pdf,tex,...} and
44	    write ONE pointer file references/<slug>.md registering it, then
45	    add it to references/summary.md. I do not delete or rewrite
46	    existing reference entries.
47	---
48	
49	# Reference Retriever
50	
51	You fetch the **original source files** — papers, books, online mathematical content — from the web and save them under the project's `references/` directory, then register each with a lightweight pointer file. You exist so the plan agent and blueprint-writers can read and quote the genuine source verbatim, rather than relying on a paraphrase (which drifts) or the dispatcher's memory (which fabricates).
52	
53	The single most important rule: **download the real file. Do not substitute a summary for it.** A paraphrase introduces exactly the errors (wrong lemma numbers, wrong tags, subtly mis-stated hypotheses) that this subagent exists to eliminate.
54	
55	## Your Job
56	
57	Your directive names one or more topics, mathematical questions, or specific sources (by arXiv ID, DOI, title + author, or URL). For each, you:
58	
59	1. Locate the source on the web (`WebSearch` + `WebFetch` for discovery).
60	2. **Download the original file(s)** with `curl`/`wget` via `Bash` into `references/`. Prefer machine-readable source (TeX/LaTeX) when it is openly available, AND keep the PDF too.
61	3. **Verify** the download is the real document, not an HTML paywall/error page saved under a `.pdf` name.
62	4. Write a lightweight **pointer file** `references/<slug>.md`: citation, local path(s), a contents/section map (table of contents), and caveats. **No paraphrased content.**
63	5. Register the new entry in `references/summary.md`.
64	6. Report back to the dispatcher with a one-line status + the report path.
65	
66	You do **not** hallucinate citations or content. If a source cannot be obtained through any legitimate open channel, you report that honestly. The dispatcher prefers an honest "not found" over a fabricated or paraphrased stand-in every time.
67	
68	## Directive Format
69	
70	```markdown
71	# Reference Retriever Directive
72	
73	## Slug
74	<slug>
75	
76	## Topic
77	<one or two sentences naming the mathematical area + the specific question>
78	
79	## What the dispatcher will use this for
80	<one paragraph: which chapter / strategy decision the source will inform, so I can prioritise which sections to map and which formats to chase>
81	
82	## Seeds (optional)
83	- arXiv: 1234.5678
84	- DOI: 10.xxxx/yyyy
85	- Title + author: "Some Paper" by Smith, year
86	- URL: https://...
87	- Search query: "<terms the dispatcher recommends>"
88	
89	(If no seeds are given, I search for the topic on arXiv + Google + Stacks Project + nLab as appropriate to the area.)
90	
91	## Out of scope
92	<sources or angles the dispatcher does NOT want — e.g. "no philosophy-of-math sources", "skip historical surveys, want technical content only">
93	
94	## Contents-map depth expected
95	- shallow: top-level table of contents only
96	- medium: section/chapter map with the relevant section(s) flagged + page numbers (default)
97	- deep: also map the specific theorems/lemmas the dispatcher named, with their exact location (section + page) inside the downloaded file — but still NO paraphrase of their content
98	```
99	
100	If the directive omits "Contents-map depth expected", default to **medium**.
101	
102	## What you do
103	
104	1. **Read your directive completely.**
105	
106	2. **Locate the source.**
107	   - If seeds are given, resolve them first.
108	   - Otherwise, `WebSearch` with focused queries derived from the topic. Prefer:
109	     - arXiv for recent technical papers.
110	     - Stacks Project (https://stacks.math.columbia.edu) for algebraic geometry — its TeX source is open on GitHub.
111	     - nLab (https://ncatlab.org) for category-theoretic content.
112	     - Open-access textbooks and lecture notes when the topic is foundational.
113	
114	3. **Download the original file(s)** with `Bash`. Prefer machine-readable source and keep the PDF too:
115	
116	   - **arXiv**: fetch BOTH the LaTeX e-print and the PDF.
117	     ```bash
118	     curl -fSL -o references/<slug>.tar.gz https://arxiv.org/e-print/<id>
119	     curl -fSL -o references/<slug>.pdf     https://arxiv.org/pdf/<id>
120	     ```
121	     If the e-print is a single `.tex`, name it `references/<slug>.tex` instead. You may unpack the tarball (`tar xzf`) into `references/<slug>-src/` if it helps map the contents — but keep the original archive too.
122	
123	   - **Stacks Project**: fetch the relevant `.tex` chapter verbatim from GitHub raw, so lemma/tag statements are exact (this is the fix for the paraphrase/tag-typo failure mode):
124	     ```bash
125	     curl -fSL -o references/<slug>-algebra.tex \
126	       https://raw.githubusercontent.com/stacks/stacks-project/master/algebra.tex
127	     ```
128	     Record the exact tags the dispatcher needs and confirm each tag's statement against the fetched `.tex`.
129	
130	   - **Journal / book with an open form**: download the open PDF (author homepage, institutional repository, numdam, Project Euclid open, publisher open-access, etc.).
131	
132	   - **General**:
133	     ```bash
134	     curl -fSL -o references/<slug>.pdf "<url>"
135	     ```
136	     `-f` makes curl fail on HTTP errors instead of silently saving an error page.
137	
138	4. **Verify the download is real**, not a paywall/login/error page wearing a `.pdf` extension:
139	   ```bash
140	   ls -l references/<slug>.pdf
141	   file references/<slug>.pdf      # expect "PDF document", not "HTML document"
142	   ```
143	   A near-empty file, an HTML MIME type, or a "log in to continue" body all mean the fetch FAILED — delete the bad file (`rm references/<slug>.pdf`) and treat that source as not obtained. **Never** rename or keep an unverified download.
144	
145	5. **Write the pointer file** `references/<slug>.md` (format below) in BOTH the success and failure cases:
146	   - **On success:** it points at the real downloaded file(s) and records the exact URL each was retrieved from. It does NOT reproduce the source's content.
147	   - **On failure** (nothing could be downloaded and verified): write the pointer anyway, recording the citation, every URL/channel you tried, and why each failed (paywall, 404, HTML-only, etc.). **Do NOT write the source's mathematical content into it** — a failure pointer carries the attempt log, never a paraphrase or recollection of what the source "probably says."
148	
149	6. **Register the entry** in `references/summary.md`. Append (do not delete existing entries) a one-line index entry referencing the slug.
150	
151	7. **Verify the files are on disk** (`ls -l references/<slug>.*`) before reporting back.
152	
153	## Acquiring paywalled / hard-to-find sources
154	
155	Many sources that look paywalled have a legitimate open copy. Before giving up, try, in order:
156	
157	- **arXiv** for the same title/authors — journal papers very often have an arXiv preprint with identical statements.
158	- **The author's personal / institutional homepage** — search `"<title>" <author> pdf` and `<author> publications`.
159	- **Open repositories**: numdam.org (French journals, seminars — this is where Grothendieck's FGA and Séminaire Bourbaki live), Project Euclid open content, institutional repositories, course pages.
160	- **`filetype:pdf` web searches**: `WebSearch` for `"<exact title>" filetype:pdf`. Some hits open a PDF directly — fetch and verify it.
161	- **Open TeX source**: Stacks Project (GitHub), many lecture notes and books publish LaTeX source openly.
162	
163	**Do NOT** use shadow-library / piracy sites (e.g. Sci-Hub, Library Genesis). Stick to legitimately open copies. If, after these steps, only a paywalled copy exists, fetch whatever open metadata you can (official TOC, abstract) into the pointer file's contents map, mark the source `PAYWALLED — no open copy located`, and say so plainly in your report. Do not paraphrase the body to compensate.
164	
165	## Pointer file format
166	
167	`references/<slug>.md` is an index card, not a summary. Keep it short.
168	
169	### Success — file(s) downloaded and verified
170	
171	```markdown
172	# <Title>
173	
174	## Citation
175	<author(s)>, "<title>", <venue / publisher>, <year>. <DOI / arXiv ID / URL>.
176	
177	## Slug
178	<slug>
179	
180	## Retrieval status
181	RETRIEVED — <date>
182	
183	## Local source files
184	- references/<slug>.pdf — PDF, VERIFIED via `file` — retrieved from <successful URL>
185	- references/<slug>.tex — LaTeX source, VERIFIED via `file` — retrieved from <successful URL>   ← if obtained
186	(List every file you saved and the EXACT URL it came from. These successful links are
187	the most important field — a later planner uses them to re-fetch or double-check.)
188	
189	## Why this source
190	<2-3 sentences: which Archon chapter / strategy decision will use this. Copy from the directive's "What the dispatcher will use this for".>
191	
192	## Contents map
193	<A table of contents / section map pointing INTO the downloaded file, so a planner knows where to look. Use the source's own numbering. NO paraphrase of the mathematics — just locations.>
194

for the genus-0 Jacobian arm) and the FGA Picard/Quot/Hilbert representability engine
(Route A for the positive-genus object).

The three source files already exist in `references/` (added by the user this iter):

- `references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf`  (Hartshorne, *Algebraic Geometry*, GTM 52)

  *Fundamental Algebraic Geometry: Grothendieck's FGA Explained*, AMS Math. Surveys 123)

Your job is NOT to fetch them. Your job is to:

The slugs to use (= existing filename stems):

(`references/abelian-varieties.pdf`, already in tree) — the planner needs to know which

# Strategy Critic Directive

## Slug
route-c-architecture

## What to read (and ONLY this)
- `/home/archon/FormalizationProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/.archon/STRATEGY.md` (verbatim — the current strategy).
- `/home/archon/FormalizationProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/references/summary.md` (the reference index).
- The blueprint chapter list + one-line topics below.
- The project goal below.

Do NOT read iter sidecars, PROGRESS.md, task_pending/done, prover results, or review
reports. Your value is a fresh mathematician's read of the strategy, not the project's
momentum.

## Project goal (one paragraph)
Formalize Christian Merten's Jacobian challenge: construct, for a smooth proper
geometrically irreducible curve `C` over an arbitrary field `k` (NO `C(k) ≠ ∅`
hypothesis on the protected signature), an Albanese/Jacobian group-scheme object `J`
together with its universal property `isAlbaneseFor` (every pointed morphism from `C`
to a smooth proper group scheme factors uniquely through `J`). End-state: zero inline
`sorry`, kernel-only axioms. The spine is pointed-vs-unpointed; the witness object is
real on both arms (genus-0 object = trivial `Spec k`; positive-genus object =
`Pic⁰_{C/k}`, a dim-`g` abelian variety, required even when `C(k)=∅`).

## Blueprint chapters (title / topic)
- `Cohomology_*` — sheaf/cohomology infrastructure (H⁰/H¹ of `O_C`, Mayer–Vietoris).
- `Differentials.tex` — Kähler differentials infrastructure.
- `Genus.tex` — arithmetic genus `dim_k H¹(C,O_C)`.
- `AlgebraicJacobian_Cotangent_GrpObj.tex` — group-object cotangent material (off-path).
- `RigidityKbar.tex` — genus-0 rigidity over `k̄` (`rigidity_over_kbar`) + chart-algebra
  envelope; currently disclosed as a gated NAMED GAP; differential/`df=0` route demoted
  to fallback (a).
- `Rigidity.tex` — scheme-level rigidity packaging (`ext_of_eqOnOpen`).
- `Jacobian.tex` — the witness construction (`nonempty_jacobianWitness`,
  `genusZeroWitness`, `positiveGenusWitness`) + the route-(c) AV-rigidity stack blocks
  (theorem of the cube → rigidity → rational-map-extends → unirational⟹constant →
  genus0-curve-to-AV), at statement+Milne-citation level.
- `AbelJacobi.tex` — Abel–Jacobi map / functoriality (`Jacobian.ofCurve`).

## New since last strategy review
The user has added three canonical sources to `references/` this iteration:
- **Mumford, *Abelian Varieties* (1970)** — the canonical source for the theorem of the
  cube + rigidity lemma (Milne's notes, already in tree, cite Mumford as canonical).
- **Hartshorne, *Algebraic Geometry*** — genus-0 curve with a rational point ≅ ℙ¹;
  `Ω_{ℙ¹} ≅ O(−2)`, `H⁰(ℙ¹, Ω) = 0`.
- **FGA Explained** — collected Kleiman (Picard) + Nitsure (Quot/Hilbert), backs Route A.

## What I want challenged (be a fresh, skeptical mathematician)
1. **Is route (c) the right next build?** STRATEGY commits the genus-0 arm to a
   characteristic-free AV-rigidity stack (theorem of the cube → Rigidity → Thm 3.2 →
   Prop 3.10), estimated ~1500–3500 LOC of abelian-variety theory that is ABSENT from
   Mathlib. The theorem of the cube is itself a deep formalization (seesaw, flat
   cohomology base change). Question hard: given Hartshorne is now in tree, is there a
   materially CHEAPER sufficient path for the genus-0 arm specifically — e.g. over `k̄`
   the curve is `ℙ¹`, and "every morphism `ℙ¹ → A` is constant" might be reachable from
   the concrete `H⁰(ℙ¹, Ω_{ℙ¹}) = H⁰(ℙ¹, O(−2)) = 0` (elementary, NOT general Serre
   duality) plus the converse `df=0 ⟹ constant` envelope the project already has closed
   (KDM/chart-algebra)? The iter-155 refutation of the `df=0` route was for a *general*
   genus-0 curve via chart-by-chart KDM; does it actually rule out the *concrete `ℙ¹`*
   computation? Or is the full theorem-of-the-cube stack genuinely the minimal route?
   I am NOT asking you to settle the math — I am asking whether STRATEGY has under-tested
   the cheapest-route question before committing to a 1500–3500 LOC AV-theory build.
2. **Risk concentration.** Both the genus-0 arm (route c, AV rigidity) and the
   positive-genus arm (Route A, FGA representability) now require building large amounts
   of absent-from-Mathlib geometry. Is the "genus-0 is the low-risk half" claim still
   credible once theorem-of-the-cube is costed honestly, or are BOTH arms multi-thousand-
   LOC research builds? Is the project's overall feasibility framing honest?
3. **Architecture.** The route-(c) rigidity declarations must live UPSTREAM of
   `Jacobian.lean` (an import cycle currently blocks `genusZeroWitness` from consuming
   `rigidity_over_kbar`, which sits downstream in `RigidityKbar.lean`). The plan is a new
   upstream Lean file for the AV rigidity stack. Any structural concern with that?
4. **Format / hygiene** of STRATEGY.md itself (per the canonical skeleton: phases table,
   routes, open questions, gaps; under ~250 lines).

## Output
Per your descriptor: SOUND / CHALLENGE / REJECT per claim, with the cheapest signal that
would change your verdict. Write to your `task_results/` report.

# Blueprint Reviewer Directive

## Slug
iter157

Audit the WHOLE blueprint at
`/home/archon/FormalizationProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/blueprint/`
(chapters under `blueprint/src/chapters/`, assembled via `blueprint/src/content.tex`).
Produce your standard per-chapter checklist (complete? correct? Lean targets well-formed?
proofs detailed enough to formalize? broken `\uses`/`\cref`?) and the HARD-GATE verdict
per chapter.

## Focus this iter (do not let it narrow the whole-blueprint sweep)
The genus-0 critical path has committed to **route (c)**: a characteristic-free
abelian-variety rigidity stack (theorem of the cube → Rigidity Theorem → rational-map-
extends → unirational⟹constant → genus-0-curve-to-AV constant). These blocks currently
live in `Jacobian.tex` (subsection `sec:av_rigidity_route_c`,
labels `thm:theorem_of_the_cube`, `lem:rigidity_theorem`,
`cor:complete_product_to_AV_decomp`, `lem:rational_map_to_AV_extends`,
`prop:unirational_to_AV_constant`, `prop:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV`) at
statement + Milne-citation level.

For these route-(c) blocks specifically, judge:
1. **Are the proofs prover-ready?** They currently cite Milne's terse notes. The project
   has just added Mumford's *Abelian Varieties* (the canonical full-proof source) and
   Hartshorne. Is the current proof detail enough for a prover to formalize, or does each
   block (esp. theorem of the cube, rigidity theorem) need substantially more detail
   sourced from Mumford? Flag exactly which blocks are too thin.
2. **Are the `\lean{...}` targets present and well-formed?** These declarations do not yet
   exist in Lean (they will live in a NEW upstream file). Are the intended Lean names /
   signatures specified clearly enough to scaffold?
3. **`% archon:covers` hygiene.** `RigidityKbar.tex` declares
   `% archon:covers RigidityKbar.lean Cotangent/ChartAlgebra.lean` — the blueprint-doctor
   flags these as nonexistent because it resolves paths relative to the repo root while
   they are written relative to `AlgebraicJacobian/`. Confirm the correct fix is to write
   full paths (`AlgebraicJacobian/RigidityKbar.lean`, `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/ChartAlgebra.lean`).
   Also assess: should the route-(c) AV-rigidity stack be a separate chapter, or is a
   consolidated `% archon:covers` on `Jacobian.tex` (covering both `Jacobian.lean` and the
   new AV-rigidity file) the cleaner choice?

## Carry-over items to confirm resolved or still-live
- iter-156 `% NOTE:` items in `Jacobian.tex` `def:genusZeroWitness`: (1) the uniqueness
  paragraph justifies uniqueness by the terminal-object property (mathematically loose;
  the sound argument is epi-cancellation of `toUnit C`); (2) the C.2.f descent is billed
  "∼2 lines" but is actually a multi-iter base-change sub-build. Are these still live?
- Cross-chapter consistency: does `RigidityKbar.tex` still frame the cotangent/`df=0` route
  as the live path while `Jacobian.tex` has moved to route (c)? Flag the mismatch.

## Output
Your standard per-chapter checklist + HARD-GATE table + the focus findings above. Write to
your `task_results/` report.

1	# Strategy Critic Report
2	
3	## Slug
4	route-c-architecture
5	
6	## Iteration
7	157
8	
9	## Routes audited
10	
11	### Route: A — Picard/Quot/Hilbert FGA engine (positive-genus object)
12	
13	- **Goal-alignment**: PASS — the protected goal forbids `C(k)≠∅`, so the positive-genus
14	  witness must be a real dim-`g` AV even when `C(k)=∅`; `Pic⁰_{C/k}` is the canonical
15	  such object and the FGA engine is genuinely unavoidable. No subtler object dodges it.
16	- **Mathematical soundness**: PASS — FGA Picard representability + identity component is
17	  established mathematics; the decomposition (Quot/Hilbert → repr `Pic` → `Pic⁰` →
18	  Albanese UP) is the standard construction.
19	- **Effort honesty**: under-counted (disclosed) — `~5100+ · 0/it` with `~40–70` iters
20	  left and **0 realized LOC after 157 iterations** is the headline risk. The line "NOT
21	  yet itemised in ~5100 — true budget higher" (the Albanese UP) is honest, but it means
22	  the *named* critical-path number is acknowledged as an under-count. Verdict honest, not
23	  dishonest, but the reader should register: the critical path has not been started.
24	- **Verdict**: SOUND — proceed, with eyes open that this is the project-fatal piece and
25	  it remains at zero realized progress.
26	
27	### Route: genus-0 rigidity (c) — char-free AV-rigidity stack
28	
29	- **Goal-alignment**: PARTIAL — the stack delivers "ℙ¹ → A is constant" (Prop 3.10), but
30	  `rigidity_over_kbar` is stated for a *general genus-0 curve* `C_k̄`. The bridge
31	  `genus-0 + k̄-point ⟹ C_k̄ ≅ ℙ¹` (or at least `⟹ unirational`) is a real, separate
32	  theorem (Riemann–Roch / anticanonical, Hartshorne IV) that is **not itemised** in the
33	  `1500–3500` budget. For a smooth proper curve, unirational ⇔ rational ⇔ `≅ ℙ¹`, so this
34	  cannot be dodged. Flag it as a missing line-item, not a soundness hole.
35	- **Mathematical soundness**: PASS — the Milne chain (cube → Rigidity 1.1 → Thm 3.2 →
36	  Prop 3.10) is correct and, crucially, **char-free**. Thm 3.2 (rational map extends) is
37	  in fact trivial for the curve case (a rational map from a smooth curve to a proper
38	  variety is automatically a morphism), so the real weight is theorem-of-the-cube +
39	  Prop 3.10.
40	- **Effort honesty**: under-counted — see CHALLENGE below. The theorem of the cube drags
41	  in **seesaw + flat/proper cohomology base change + semicontinuity + line bundles on
42	  products**, none of which exist in Mathlib (verified: no `AbelianVariety`, no Picard
43	  infrastructure, only bare `Proj` structure sheaf). These prerequisites are plausibly
44	  comparable in cost to a chunk of the representability monster, yet the row is labelled
45	  "the LOW-risk half" and "likely the cheapest unbuilt critical-path deliverable."
46	- **Verdict**: CHALLENGE — the *commitment* to route (c) is defensible (the char-free
47	  property is a genuine, non-sunk-cost reason; see below), but the **cost framing is not
48	  honest** and the **cheapest-route comparison is asserted, not shown**. Both must be
49	  fixed in STRATEGY.md this iter.
50	
51	### Route: genus-0 rigidity (a) — differential / Serre-duality fallback
52	
53	- **Goal-alignment**: PARTIAL — would close genus-0 only in `CharZero`; the protected
54	  goal is arbitrary `[Field k]`. Correctly demoted to off-path fallback.
55	- **Mathematical soundness**: PASS — sound where it applies (char 0).
56	- **Verdict**: SOUND — correctly held as a gated fallback, not deleted.
57	
58	### Route: genus-0 rigidity (b) — byproduct of the engine
59	
60	- **Verdict**: SOUND — `Alb(genus-0)=Pic⁰(ℙ¹)=0` is correct; correctly ranked behind (c)
61	  because it couples genus-0 to the A.2 representability risk.
62	
63	## Format compliance
64	
65	- **Size**: 134 lines / ~9 KB — within budget.
66	- **Headings**: PASS — section list is exactly `## Goal`, `## Phases & estimations`,
67	  `## Routes`, `## Open strategic questions`, `## Mathlib gaps & new material`, in order.
68	- **Per-iter narrative detected**: no (borderline) — no literal `iter-NNN` strings. Mild
69	  history-flavoured phrases ("skeleton already 6/7 closed", "the `df=0` framing was a red
70	  herring of the chart route", "Kept in tree … not deleted") are present but do not name
71	  iterations. Trim on next edit; not a violation.
72	- **Accumulation detected**: no — the "New project material introduced (in tree)"
73	  bullet list edges toward inventory-tracking, but is framed as gaps/assets and is short.
74	- **Table discipline**: PASS — columns match the canonical set; LOC cells carry both
75	  figures (e.g. `~5100+ · 0/it`).
76	- **Appendix sections**: the trailing `**Soundness rules (operational):**` is a bolded
77	  paragraph inside the last section, not a `##` heading — not the renamed-section
78	  violation the skeleton warns about. Acceptable; could fold into a one-liner.
79	- **Format verdict**: COMPLIANT.
80	
81	## Alternative routes (suggested)
82	
83	### Alternative: concrete-ℙ¹ differential route (the directive's question 1)
84	
85	- **What it looks like**: over `k̄`, `C_k̄ ≅ ℙ¹`; compute `H⁰(ℙ¹,Ω) = H⁰(ℙ¹,O(−2)) = 0`
86	  *directly* (Euler sequence / explicit transition functions — NOT general Serre
87	  duality); pull back invariant differentials `f^*: H⁰(A,Ω_A) → H⁰(ℙ¹,Ω) = 0`; since
88	  invariant forms generate `Ω_A` (the `GrpObj` cotangent material), conclude `df = 0`;
89	  then `df=0 ⟹ constant` via the **already-closed, axiom-clean KDM/chart envelope**.
90	- **Why it might be cheaper or sounder**: it reuses an asset the project already owns and
91	  sidesteps the theorem of the cube entirely. The directive's sharp sub-question is
92	  correct: the iter-155 refutation was about a *general* genus-0 curve where every chart
93	  `Ω_C(V)` is free rank-1 so KDM can't *detect* `df=0` globally — that refutation does
94	  **not** apply to the *concrete* `ℙ¹`, whose `H⁰(Ω)=0` is genuinely elementary. So the
95	  "cheap ℙ¹" route is **not** killed by iter-155.
96	- **Why it nonetheless does not displace route (c)**: it dies on the **char-`p` wall**.
97	  `df=0 ⟹ constant` is FALSE in char `p` without Frobenius descent (a map can factor
98	  through the Frobenius `ℙ¹→ℙ¹`). Closing it needs: (1) `df=0 ⟹ factors through Frobenius`
99	  (a real theorem to formalize), and (2) the infinite-factorisation argument
100	  `f^*k(A) ⊆ ⋂_n k(ℙ¹)^{p^n} = k̄ ⟹ constant`. That is a whole Frobenius-descent
101	  subproject the protected no-`CharZero` goal forces. Route (c) is char-free precisely to
102	  avoid this. **This is the real reason route (c) wins, and STRATEGY.md never states it**
103	  — it lumps the cheap-ℙ¹ idea under "fallback (a) carries a char-`p` gap" without noting
104	  that ℙ¹'s `H⁰(Ω)=0` is cheap (unlike general Serre duality). The cheapest-route question
105	  has been *answered correctly but not shown to be tested*.
106	- **What the current strategy may have rejected**: it conflates "concrete ℙ¹ `H⁰=0`" with
107	  "general Serre duality `H⁰(C,Ω_C)=0`" and discards both together under (a).
108	- **Severity of the omission**: major — the conclusion (commit c) is right, but the
109	  decision record must show the comparison, or a fresh reader cannot tell route (c) was
110	  chosen on merit rather than on the cube being already half-blueprinted.
111	
112	## Prerequisite verification
113	
114	- `AbelianVariety` (Mathlib AV theory): MISSING (loogle: no results) — consistent with
115	  the strategy's "absent from Mathlib" claim. Safe direction (asserts absence).
116	- `PicardScheme` / `Pic⁰` representability: MISSING — consistent with strategy.
117	- `RelativeSpec` (named A.2 entry point, ~700–1100 LOC): MISSING under that name (loogle:
118	  no results). Mathlib has `Spec`/`StructureSheaf`/`Proj` but no relative-Spec packaging
119	  surfaced. Not phantom-assumed-present (strategy treats it as a build target), but the
120	  exact entry-point name should be confirmed before a prover is gated on it.
121	- ℙ¹ differential cohomology (`H⁰(ℙ¹,Ω)=0`, `Ω_{ℙ¹}≅O(−2)`): MISSING — only bare `Proj`
122	  structure-sheaf machinery exists. Relevant if the concrete-ℙ¹ alternative is ever
123	  costed: that route is NOT free either.
124	
125	## Must-fix-this-iter
126	
127	- **Route (c): CHALLENGE** — (1) cost the **theorem-of-the-cube prerequisites** (seesaw,
128	  flat/proper cohomology base change, semicontinuity, line bundles on products) as
129	  explicit sub-line-items, and confirm the total is genuinely cheaper than the
130	  df=0+Frobenius-descent alternative; (2) add the missing line-item
131	  `genus-0 curve over k̄ ⟹ ≅ ℙ¹ / unirational` (Riemann–Roch, Hartshorne) to the genus-0
132	  budget; (3) either soften "the LOW-risk half / cheapest unbuilt deliverable" or justify
133	  it against the now-explicit cube cost.
134	- **Alternative (concrete-ℙ¹): major** — record in STRATEGY.md the one-line reason route
135	  (c) beats the cheap-ℙ¹ route: *ℙ¹'s `H⁰(Ω)=0` is elementary and iter-155 does not refute
136	  it, but the df=0 step needs Frobenius descent in char `p`, which the char-free cube
137	  avoids.* This converts an asserted decision into a tested one.
138	
139	## Overall verdict
140	
141	A fresh mathematician would **endorse the destination but not the map**. The commitment
142	to route (c) for genus-0 is the correct call, and for the right underlying reason — the
143	protected goal's arbitrary `[Field k]` makes char-freeness decisive, since every
144	differential/`H⁰` route (including the genuinely-cheap concrete-ℙ¹ computation that
145	iter-155 does *not* refute) hits a char-`p` Frobenius-descent wall that the
146	theorem-of-the-cube stack sidesteps. But STRATEGY.md presents this as settled without
147	showing the comparison, and it under-costs route (c) by hiding the theorem-of-the-cube
148	prerequisites (seesaw + cohomology base change, all absent from Mathlib) and the
149	`genus-0 ⟹ ℙ¹` bridge inside a flat `1500–3500` estimate while branding it "the low-risk
150	half." With BOTH arms now confirmed as multi-thousand-LOC, absent-from-Mathlib geometry
151	builds at 0 realized LOC/iter, "genus-0 is low-risk" is true only *relative to*
152	representability, not in absolute terms — the overall feasibility framing should say so.
153	Format is clean and compliant. Fix the two recorded items in-place this iter; the routes
154	themselves do not need rewriting.
155

40	- **Route genus-0 rigidity: STUCK** — primary corrective: **structural refactor** (the import-cycle-breaking scaffold), which the planner has ALREADY chosen — so the must-fix is not "pick a corrective" but "**bind the corrective to a hard checkpoint**": iter-157 builds the importable scaffold; **iter-158 MUST fire a prover at the scaffolded `genusZeroWitness.key` / `rigidity_over_kbar` declaration.** If iter-158 is another plan/blueprint/refactor round with no prover dispatch on this route, the route flips to CHURNING (plan-phase-only meta-pattern) and the corrective escalates to route-pivot / cheaper-route search. Record this checkpoint in `iter/iter-157/plan.md`.
41	- **Route genus-0 rigidity: throughput honesty** — the phase-clock reset at the 156 pivot makes the route read ON_SCHEDULE while the keystone is ~8 iters old across two retired framings. The planner should record the *cumulative* keystone budget (≈8 elapsed + 6–12 estimated) somewhere visible, so route (c) is recognized as the LAST affordable framing before user-escalation, not a fresh start.
42	
43	## Overall verdict
44	
45	Two critical-path routes, neither healthy in the converging sense: route 1 (genus-0) is **STUCK** by the verbatim rules — its keystone has resisted closure for ~8 iters across two framings. But this is *managed* stuck, not blind churn: iters 155–156 did real, falsifiable diagnostic work (the mathlib-analogist consult REFUTED the df=0 route on solid grounds; the 156 pivot VERIFIED three concrete structural blockers), and iter-157's scaffold directly addresses the verified import-cycle blocker. The "no prover, build the scaffold" call is correct as a one-time prerequisite, not a stall. The danger is that route (c) is the THIRD framing of the keystone and is itself a large theorem-of-the-cube formalization that could stall for many iters. The planner's iter should therefore: (1) execute the scaffold refactor as planned, (2) **commit to a hard prover checkpoint at iter-158** so plan-only does not metastasize, and (3) expand the route-(c) blueprint to prover-ready granularity (and resolve the `[CharZero]`/char-free tension) so iter-158's prover does not run into the same under-specification wall that sank df=0. Route 2 (positive-genus) is correctly dormant — UNCLEAR/not-started, no action.
46

Both critics converge powerfully. Key takeaways:

- **strategy-critic: CHALLENGE on route (c)** — the cost framing is dishonest (theorem-of-the-cube drags in seesaw + cohomology base change + semicontinuity, all absent from Mathlib; plus an un-itemized `genus-0 ⟹ ℙ¹` Riemann–Roch bridge). But the *commitment* is correct, for a reason STRATEGY never states: **char-freeness**. The cheap concrete-ℙ¹ differential route (which iter-155 does NOT actually refute) dies on a char-`p` Frobenius wall; the cube route sidesteps it. Crucially flags that the **Rigidity Theorem itself is cube-free**.
- **progress-critic: STUCK (managed)** — endorses the scaffold-refactor corrective but binds a **hard checkpoint: iter-158 MUST fire a prover** at the scaffolded declaration, else CHURNING.

Let me wait for the remaining two reports (reference-retriever, blueprint-reviewer).

71	   **Exercise IV.1.3** (same page, doc p.297).

input.content:
# Blueprint Writer Directive

## Slug
av-rigidity-chapter

## Target chapter
blueprint/src/chapters/AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex  (NEW FILE — create it)

This chapter is the dedicated home for the project's **committed genus-0 route (c)**: the
characteristic-free abelian-variety rigidity stack. The blueprint-reviewer (iter157)
directed that this stack get its OWN chapter (1:1 with a new upstream Lean file
`AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`), NOT a consolidated cover on Jacobian.tex.

At the very top of the new chapter, after `\chapter{...}\label{chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity}`,
declare coverage with the FULL repo-root-relative path:

    % archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean

## Strategy context (the slice that matters)
The genus-0 Jacobian arm has a TRIVIAL witness object (`J = Spec k`); its only remaining
mathematical content is a rigidity STATEMENT: "every pointed morphism from a genus-0 curve
over `k̄` to a smooth proper group scheme (abelian variety) `A` is constant (factors through
the identity point)." This is consumed by `genusZeroWitness.key` in `Jacobian.lean` (after a
`k̄ → k` descent step that lives in Jacobian.tex, NOT here).

The project commits to proving it CHARACTERISTIC-FREE, because the protected goal is over an
arbitrary `[Field k]`. (The cheaper differential `df=0` route — via the elementary
`H⁰(ℙ¹,Ω)=H⁰(ℙ¹,O(−2))=0` — dies on a char-`p` Frobenius-descent wall; the rigidity-lemma
route sidesteps it. Do NOT use the `df=0`/Serre framing in this chapter.)

## Required content — the MINIMAL chain (your first task is to determine it)

The chain, from Mumford + Hartshorne + Milne (all in tree). **Your most important job:
determine from MUMFORD whether `ℙ¹ → A constant` needs the theorem of the cube, or only the
cube-free Rigidity Lemma.** The reference-retriever found that Mumford's Rigidity Lemma
(Form I, §4 p.43) is a properness/closedness argument that does NOT use the cube, and that
Mumford leaves "no rational curves / `ℙ¹→A` constant" as a CONSEQUENCE of the rigidity lemma
+ corollaries — i.e. likely cube-free for a single `ℙ¹` factor (the cube / Cor 1.5 enters
only the multi-factor unirational induction, which a single curve avoids). Read Mumford §4
(PDF pp.54–56) and confirm or refute this. Structure the chapter around the MINIMAL chain:

1. **`thm:rigidity_lemma`** — `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_lemma}`. The Rigidity Lemma:
   let `V` be a complete (proper) variety, `W` any variety, both over `k`, with `V × W`
   geometrically irreducible and a point `(v₀,w₀)`; let `α : V × W → Z` be a morphism with
   `α(V × {w₀})` a single point. Then `α` factors through the projection `V × W → W` (i.e.
   `α` depends only on the `W`-coordinate). PROVER-READY detail: this is the iter-158 prover
   entry, so give the full proof (Mumford Form I, §4 p.43 / Milne Thm 1.1): pick an affine
   open `U ∋ α(v₀,w₀)`; `V` complete ⟹ the projection `q : V × W → W` is closed; the set of
   `w` with `α(V × {w}) ⊆ U` is open and nonempty; on it `α(V × {w})` is a complete connected
   subvariety of an affine, hence a point; conclude `α` is constant on each `V`-fibre over a
   dense open, then everywhere by separatedness/irreducibility. Spell out each step.

2. **`prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant`** — `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const}`.
   Every morphism `f : ℙ¹_{k̄} → A` to an abelian variety (smooth proper group scheme) is
   constant. Derive from `thm:rigidity_lemma` (Milne Prop 3.10's packaged corollary). Give the
   proof at the granularity Mumford/Milne use; if it genuinely needs the cube, state the cube
   as `thm:theorem_of_the_cube` and `\uses` it, but PREFER the cube-free derivation if you can
   confirm one from Mumford. (If you keep the cube, mark `thm:theorem_of_the_cube` clearly as a
   DEFERRED deep input with no full proof yet, and add a `% NOTE:`-free prose flag that it is
   the heavy un-built prerequisite.)

3. **`prop:genusZero_curve_iso_P1`** — `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.genusZero_curve_iso_P1}`. A
   smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve of genus 0 over `k̄` (which has a `k̄`-rational
   point) is isomorphic to `ℙ¹_{k̄}`. Source: Hartshorne Example IV.1.3.5 / Exercise IV.1.3
   (genus = `dim H¹(O)`, Prop IV.1.1; Riemann–Roch gives a degree-1 divisor ⟹ iso to `ℙ¹`).
   This is Riemann–Roch-flavoured and Mathlib has no Riemann–Roch — so write the statement +
   proof SKETCH at textbook level (cite Hartshorne), and flag in the prose that its
   formalization is itself a sub-build (do not pretend it is short). `\lean{}` target present.

4. **`thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV`** — `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme}`.
   THE HEADLINE the genus-0 arm consumes. A pointed morphism `f : C → A` (with `f` killing the
   marked point) from a smooth proper geometrically irreducible genus-0 curve `C` over `k̄` to a
   smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme `A` over `k̄` equals the constant
   morphism at the identity. Proof: compose `prop:genusZero_curve_iso_P1` with
   `prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant`; the pointed hypothesis pins the constant value to the
   identity. Characteristic-free: `[IsAlgClosed k̄]` only, NO `[CharZero]`. Note explicitly that
   this is the char-free replacement for the existing `rigidity_over_kbar` (which carries
   `[CharZero]` and lives in RigidityKbar.lean as the fallback-(a) artifact).

### Lean signature guidance (for the `\lean{}` targets / scaffolding)
- All declarations live in the NEW file `AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`,
  which will import `AlgebraicJacobian.Genus` and be imported by `AlgebraicJacobian.Jacobian`
  (this breaks the current `RigidityKbar → Rigidity → Jacobian` import cycle that blocks
  `genusZeroWitness` from consuming the keystone).
- `thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV`'s signature should mirror the existing
  `AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_over_kbar` (in `RigidityKbar.lean`) MINUS the `[CharZero k̄]`
  instance — same curve/group-scheme typeclasses, same pointed-morphism conclusion shape
  (`f = toUnit C ≫ η[A]`). You do NOT write Lean; just describe the intended statement clearly
  enough that a scaffolder can transcribe the signature. Mention the `rigidity_over_kbar`
  signature as the template.

## Citation discipline — IMPORTANT, the sources are SCANNED images
Per the reference-retriever cards:
- `references/mumford-abelian-varieties.pdf` — SCANNED, **no text layer**. Rigidity Lemma
  (Form I) + corollaries at **PDF page 54** (book §4 p.43); AV definition/conventions PDF p.50
  (book p.39); Theorem of the Cube **PDF page 66** (book §6 p.55). Offset = book + 11.
- `references/hartshorne-algebraic-geometry.pdf` — SCANNED, no text layer. genus-0 ≅ ℙ¹
  **PDF page 314** (Example IV.1.3.5, doc p.297) + Exercise IV.1.3; genus = dim H¹(O) Prop
  IV.1.1 PDF p.311. Offset = doc + 17.
- `references/abelian-varieties.pdf` (Milne) — SCANNED. Prop 3.10 at **PDF page 26**; Rigidity
  Thm 1.1 PDF p.14.

To produce verbatim `% SOURCE QUOTE:` text you MUST open the actual PDF page with the Read
tool's `pages` parameter (e.g. `pages: "54"` on the Mumford PDF) and TRANSCRIBE the statement
from the rendered page image. Do NOT quote from memory and do NOT quote the `.md` pointer
cards (they carry no quotable text). Each block needs: `% SOURCE: <pointer>, <loc> (read from
references/<file>.pdf)`, a verbatim `% SOURCE QUOTE:` (and `% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:` before the
proof env where the source gives a proof), and a visible `\textit{Source: ...}` first line.
If a specific page genuinely will not render, flag that block `% SOURCE: ... (verbatim text
not yet retrieved — page did not render)` and leave the statement in project notation without
a fabricated quote — do NOT invent the verbatim text.

## Macros
`\mathbb P`, `\Spec`, `\bar k`, `\genus` are already used elsewhere; `\dashrightarrow` is from
amssymb (loaded). Define any NEW macro you need in `blueprint/src/macros/common.tex`? — NO:
you may only edit your assigned chapter. If you need a new macro, use an inline `\ensuremath`
or a local `\newcommand` at the top of YOUR chapter, and note it in your report.

## Out of scope
- Do NOT edit `Jacobian.tex` or `RigidityKbar.tex` (the plan agent strips the duplicated
  route-(c) blocks from `Jacobian.tex` and reframes `RigidityKbar.tex` separately). Use the
  SAME label names the current `Jacobian.tex` `sec:av_rigidity_route_c` uses where they carry
  over (`thm:theorem_of_the_cube`, `prop:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV`) so existing `\cref`s
  resolve to your new chapter — EXCEPT introduce the new minimal-chain labels above
  (`thm:rigidity_lemma`, `prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant`, `prop:genusZero_curve_iso_P1`).

# Iter-157 plan-agent run

## Headline outcome

Iter-157 is the **route-(c) grounding + architecture-decision iter**. The user supplied
three canonical sources (Mumford *Abelian Varieties*, Hartshorne, FGA Explained); the iter
registered them, used them to harden the committed genus-0 route (c), and decided the file
architecture that breaks the import cycle blocking the keystone. The central technical
result of the iter: **the route-(c) entry is the cube-free Rigidity Lemma**, not the
theorem of the cube — a major cost reduction the strategy-critic asked for and the
reference-retriever confirmed from Mumford §4 p.43.

No critical-path proof prover this iter (mechanical gate: the route-(c) chapter is being
brought to prover-ready detail + the importable Lean target does not yet exist). Instead the
iter builds the prover-ready blueprint + (if review clears) the importable scaffold, so the
**iter-158 prover checkpoint** (bound by the progress-critic) can fire.

## Wave 1 (parallel) — 3 mandatory critics + reference-retriever

| Subagent | Slug | Verdict | Absorption |
|---|---|---|---|
| reference-retriever | register-new-sources | COMPLETE | Registered Mumford / Hartshorne / FGA (pointer cards + summary + deep page maps). KEY: Mumford Rigidity Lemma (Form I) §4 p.43 is **cube-free**; "ℙ¹→A constant" is a *consequence* of it (not separately labelled — Milne Prop 3.10 packages it). Both Mumford + Hartshorne PDFs are SCANNED (no text layer → verbatim quotes need rendered-image reads). Hartshorne genus-0≅ℙ¹ = Ex IV.1.3.5. |
| strategy-critic | route-c-architecture | **CHALLENGE (route c) + Route A SOUND** | Commitment to (c) is correct but for an unstated reason: **char-freeness**. The cheap concrete-ℙ¹ `df=0` route (iter-155 does NOT actually refute it — `H⁰(ℙ¹,Ω)=H⁰(ℙ¹,O(−2))=0` is elementary) dies on a char-`p` Frobenius wall; the cube route sidesteps it. Must-fix: cost the cube prerequisites honestly, add the genus-0⟹ℙ¹ Riemann–Roch line-item, soften "low-risk half", record the char-freeness decision. ALL addressed in STRATEGY.md this iter. |
| progress-critic | genus0-route-c | **STUCK (managed)** | Keystone open ~8 iters across 2 framings; but the iter-156 pivot + iter-157 scaffold-refactor is the correct corrective. Binds a HARD CHECKPOINT: **iter-158 MUST fire a prover** at the scaffolded declaration, else CHURNING → route-pivot/cheaper-route. Recorded below + in PROGRESS.md. |
| blueprint-reviewer | iter157 | **Jacobian.tex + RigidityKbar.tex partial (must-fix); 9/11 clean** | Route (c) committed in Jacobian.tex but un-wired, statement-thin (cube/rational-map-extends), `\lean{}`-less, and absent from RigidityKbar.tex (still df=0). Architecture call: **give route (c) its OWN chapter + upstream Lean file**, NOT a consolidated cover. Confirmed covers-path fix (full repo-root paths). |

## Decision made

**Commit the route-(c) minimal chain in a NEW dedicated chapter + upstream Lean file;
defer the theorem of the cube unless it proves unavoidable.**

- **Architecture.** New `AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean` (imports `Genus`,
  imported by `Jacobian`) + its own 1:1 chapter `AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex`. This breaks
  the `RigidityKbar → Rigidity → Jacobian` cycle that prevented `genusZeroWitness` from
  consuming the keystone. The old `rigidity_over_kbar` (in `RigidityKbar.lean`, `[CharZero]`)
  becomes the fallback-(a) artifact, kept in tree. (blueprint-reviewer's separate-chapter
  recommendation, NOT a consolidated cover — so the heavy route-(c) verdict can't gate the
  clean `Jacobian.lean` projection declarations.)
- **Minimal chain (cube-free first).** Entry = the **Rigidity Lemma** (Mumford Form I,
  cube-free properness argument), blueprinted to prover-ready detail; then `ℙ¹→A constant`
  (likely from the rigidity lemma alone), `genus-0+k̄-pt ⟹ ≅ℙ¹` (Hartshorne, flagged as a
  Riemann–Roch sub-build), and the headline `rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV` (char-free).
  Theorem of the cube kept only as a DEFERRED deep input if `ℙ¹→A` genuinely needs it.
- **Why (c) over the cheap-ℙ¹ `df=0` route:** char-freeness (the protected goal is
  arbitrary `[Field k]`; `df=0 ⟹ constant` is false in char `p` without Frobenius descent).
  Recorded in STRATEGY.md Routes (c).
- **Cheapest reversal signal:** if the blueprint-writer finds `ℙ¹→A constant` *requires*
  the full theorem of the cube (not just the rigidity lemma), the genus-0 cost balloons to
  representability-scale — at which point reconsider route (b) (engine byproduct) since the
  cube-laden (c) loses its decoupling advantage.

## Hard checkpoint (progress-critic, binding)

**iter-158 MUST dispatch a prover** at the scaffolded `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`
declaration (the Rigidity Lemma entry). If iter-158 is another plan/blueprint/refactor
round with no prover on this route, the route flips to CHURNING and the corrective
escalates to route-pivot / cheaper-route search. Cumulative keystone budget: ~8 iters
elapsed + ~6–12 estimated — route (c) is the last affordable framing before user-escalation.

## Wave 2 — blueprint-writer (new chapter)

(av-rigidity-chapter) — outcome recorded after return.

## Wave 3 — fast-path scoped re-review + refactor (scaffold)

(recorded after return.)

## Edits made by the plan agent directly
- STRATEGY.md: route-(c) phase row re-costed (cube-free rigidity lemma entry, genus-0⟹ℙ¹
  bridge, cube as deferred deep input); Routes (c) records the char-freeness decisive
  reason + cube-avoidance open question; architecture decision (new upstream file + own
  chapter); new-material entry; cumulative budget.
- content.tex: added `\input{chapters/AbelianVarietyRigidity}`.
- RigidityKbar.tex: fixed `% archon:covers` to full repo-root paths (doctor must-fix);
  added a STRATEGY NOTE redirecting the committed route to the new chapter and reframing
  this chapter as the fallback-(a) home (resolves the route-mismatch must-fix).

## Follow-ups recorded (not this iter)
- RigidityKbar.tex `thm:rigidity_over_kbar` stale Mumford `% SOURCE` (claims paywalled;
  Mumford now bundled) — refresh with a verbatim quote (fallback declaration, "soon").
- Jacobian.tex carry-over NOTEs (uniqueness epi-cancellation prose; descent re-cost) — the
  descent C.2.f remains a multi-iter base-change sub-build.

## Subagent skips
- (none — all 3 highly-recommended critics dispatched.)

-rw-r--r-- 1 archon archon 11035 May 20 17:33 .archon/task_results/blueprint-writer-av-rigidity-chapter.md
-rw-r--r-- 1 archon archon 21902 May 20 17:32 blueprint/src/chapters/AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex
BOTH PRESENT

1	# Blueprint Writer Report
2	
3	## Slug
4	av-rigidity-chapter
5	
6	## Status
7	COMPLETE — all five required blocks written and sourced; one Strategy-modifying finding (the
8	single-$\mathbb P^1$ base case is NOT cube-free, refining the iter-156 expectation).
9	
10	## Target chapter
11	blueprint/src/chapters/AbelianVarietyRigidity.tex  (NEW FILE — created)
12	
13	## Determination of the minimal chain (the directive's "most important job")
14	I read Mumford §4 (PDF p.54, the Rigidity Lemma + proof) and §6 (PDF p.66, the theorem of
15	the cube), Milne Thm 1.1 (PDF p.14) and Prop 3.10 (PDF p.26, statement + proof), and
16	Hartshorne Example IV.1.3.5 (PDF p.314). Conclusion on the cube question:
17	
18	- **The Rigidity Lemma (Mumford Form I) is cube-free** — its proof is purely a
19	  completeness/closed-map argument (`p₂` closed since `X` complete; a proper connected
20	  variety has no nonconstant map to an affine). Confirmed verbatim. It is the natural
21	  formalisation entry point.
22	- **The single-$\mathbb P^1$ constancy is NOT a consequence of the Rigidity Lemma alone.**
23	  The Rigidity Lemma collapses a *product* `X × Y → Z` when one axis is already collapsed;
24	  a bare `f : ℙ¹ → A` presents no collapsed axis (the difference map `(x,y) ↦ f(x)−f(y)` is
25	  constant on an axis only if `f` already is). Mumford himself leaves "no rational curves /
26	  `ℙ¹ → A` constant" to the reader (the reference card confirms it is *not* a labelled §4
27	  result), and Milne's Prop 3.10 proof bottoms out at "the lemma shows each `βᵢ` is
28	  constant" — i.e. the single-curve base case is taken as known. In characteristic-free
29	  form that base case rests on the **theorem of the cube** (the char-0 alternative,
30	  `f*ω` + `H⁰(ℙ¹,Ω)=0`, is exactly the Frobenius-wall route the project abandoned).
31	- **What IS cube-free is the multi-factor induction**: Milne Cor 1.5 (additivity of
32	  `Hom(−,A)` over a product of complete pointed varieties) is a corollary of the Rigidity
33	  Lemma, and drives `β(x₁,…,x_d) = Σ βᵢ(xᵢ)`. So the directive's framing was half right:
34	  the *induction* is cube-free, the *base* is not.
35	
36	I therefore structured the chapter around the minimal chain with the cube retained as a
37	clearly-flagged **deferred deep input** (matching the project memory chain "thm of cube →
38	Rigidity 1.1 → … → Prop 3.10"), and recorded the refutation in
39	`\cref{rmk:cube_is_load_bearing}` and in Strategy-modifying findings below.
40	
41	## Changes Made
42	- **Added lemma** `\label{thm:rigidity_lemma}` / `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_lemma}` —
43	  Mumford Rigidity Lemma (Form I), with the **full cube-free proof** (the iter-158 prover
44	  entry). `% SOURCE`/`% SOURCE QUOTE`/`% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF` all transcribed from the
45	  rendered Mumford PDF p.54. Does NOT `\uses` the cube.
46	- **Added remark** `\label{rmk:rigidity_lemma_cube_free}` — flags the lemma as the
47	  lowest-prerequisite link / formalisation entry point.
48	- **Added theorem** `\label{thm:theorem_of_the_cube}` — Mumford theorem of the cube,
49	  verbatim statement from PDF p.66, **no proof env** (deferred deep input, no Lean target).
50	  Re-grounded in Mumford (the directive flagged Milne's "deferred to next version" quote in

STRATEGY.md is now honest about the cube. Before the fast-path review, I must strip the duplicated route-(c) blocks from `Jacobian.tex` (they now live in the new chapter with the same labels — a clash). Let me read that region precisely.

443	\subsection{The genus-$0$ arm of the witness existence}
444	\label{sec:genusZeroWitness}
445	
446	The genus-$0$ sub-case of \cref{thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness} admits an explicit witness construction at the Lean level, packaged separately from the general existence statement so that the genus-$0$ branch can be discharged \emph{independently of Route~A}. Its witness object is the trivial scheme $\Spec k$; the only remaining mathematical content is the rigidity statement \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar}, whose committed proof route is the characteristic-free AV-rigidity lemma of \cref{sec:av_rigidity_route_c} (route~(c)), \emph{not} the differential / Serre-duality route and \emph{not} the Picard representability engine.
447	
448	\subsection{Route (c): rigidity via the theorem of the cube (committed genus-$0$ proof route)}
449	\label{sec:av_rigidity_route_c}
450	
451	The genus-$0$ witness \emph{object} is the trivial scheme $\Spec k$; the only remaining mathematical content of the genus-$0$ arm is the rigidity \emph{statement} \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar}. The project commits to proving it by the characteristic-free \emph{theorem-of-the-cube $\to$ rigidity-lemma} chain of Milne, \emph{Abelian Varieties}, recorded in the blocks below. The headline fact the genus-$0$ arm consumes is \cref{prop:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV}, the specialisation to a genus-$0$ curve of \cref{prop:unirational_to_AV_constant} (Milne, Proposition~3.10), applied to $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$ (a rational, hence unirational, variety). This chain uses \emph{neither} $H^0(C, \Omega_C) = 0$ / Serre duality \emph{nor} the FGA $\Pic$ representability engine, so the genus-$0$ arm is fully \textbf{decoupled} from Route~A. The same rigidity machinery is what the positive-genus Albanese universal property (Milne, Propositions~6.1/6.4) needs anyway, here used independently of the Picard representability.
452	
453	\begin{theorem}
454	[Theorem of the cube]
455	  \label{thm:theorem_of_the_cube}
456	  % SOURCE: [Milne, Abelian Varieties], Theorem 5.1 (Theorem of the Cube), \S I.5, p.~21 (PDF p.~27)  (read from references/abelian-varieties.pdf)
457	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "THEOREM 5.1 (THEOREM OF THE CUBE). Let $U$, $V$, $W$ be
458	  % complete geometrically irreducible varieties over $k$, and let
459	  % $u_0 \in U(k)$, $v_0 \in V(k)$, $w_0 \in W(k)$ be base points. Then an
460	  % invertible sheaf $L$ on $U \times V \times W$ is trivial if its
461	  % restrictions to $U \times V \times \{w_0\}$, $U \times \{v_0\} \times W$,
462	  % $\{u_0\} \times V \times W$ are all trivial."
463	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "We defer the proof until later in this section
464	  % [actually, until the next version]." (proof deferred in the source notes)
465	  \textit{Source: Milne, Abelian Varieties, Theorem 5.1.}
466	  Let $U$, $V$, $W$ be complete geometrically irreducible varieties over $k$ with base points $u_0 \in U(k)$, $v_0 \in V(k)$, $w_0 \in W(k)$. An invertible sheaf $L$ on $U \times V \times W$ is trivial as soon as its restrictions to the three coordinate faces $U \times V \times \{w_0\}$, $U \times \{v_0\} \times W$, and $\{u_0\} \times V \times W$ are all trivial.
467	\end{theorem}
468	
469	\begin{lemma}
470	[Rigidity Theorem]
471	  \label{lem:rigidity_theorem}
472	  % SOURCE: [Milne, Abelian Varieties], Theorem 1.1 (Rigidity Theorem), \S I.1, p.~8 (PDF p.~14--15)  (read from references/abelian-varieties.pdf)
473	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "THEOREM 1.1 (RIGIDITY THEOREM). Consider a regular map
474	  % $\alpha\colon V \times W \to U$, and assume that $V$ is complete and that
475	  % $V \times W$ is geometrically irreducible. If there are points
476	  % $u_0 \in U(k)$, $v_0 \in V(k)$, and $w_0 \in W(k)$ such that
477	  % $\alpha(V \times \{w_0\}) = \{u_0\} = \alpha(\{v_0\} \times W)$
478	  % then $\alpha(V \times W) = \{u_0\}$."
479	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Since the hypotheses continue to hold after
480	  % extending scalars from $k$ to $k^{al}$, we can assume $k$ is
481	  % algebraically closed. Note that $V$ is connected, because otherwise
482	  % $V \times_k W$ wouldn't be connected, much less irreducible. We need to
483	  % use the following facts: (i) If $V$ is complete, then the projection map
484	  % $q\colon V \times_k W \to W$ is closed (this is the definition of being
485	  % complete AG 7.1). (ii) If $V$ is complete and connected, and
486	  % $\varphi\colon V \to U$ is a regular map from $V$ into an affine variety,
487	  % then $\varphi(V) = \{$ point$\}$ (AG 7.5). Let $U_0$ be an open affine
488	  % neighbourhood of $u_0$. Because of (i), $Z =_{df} q(\alpha^{-1}(U
489	  % \smallsetminus U_0))$ is closed in $W$. By definition, $Z$ consists of the
490	  % second coordinates of points of $V \times W$ not mapping into $U_0$. Thus a
491	  % point $w$ of $W$ lies outside $Z$ if and only $\alpha(V \times \{w\})
492	  % \subseteq U_0$. In particular $w_0$ lies outside $Z$, and so $W
493	  % \smallsetminus Z$ is nonempty. As $V \times \{w\}$ is complete and $U_0$ is
494	  % affine, $\alpha(V \times \{w\})$ must be a point whenever $w \in W
495	  % \smallsetminus Z$: in fact, $\alpha(V \times \{w\}) = \alpha(v_0,w) =
496	  % \{u_0\}$. Thus $\alpha$ is constant on the subset $V \times (W
497	  % \smallsetminus Z)$ of $V \times W$. As $V \times (W \smallsetminus Z)$ is
498	  % nonempty and open in $V \times W$, and $V \times W$ is irreducible,
499	  % $V \times (W \smallsetminus Z)$ is dense $V \times W$. As $U$ is separated,
500	  % $\alpha$ must agree with the constant map on the whole of $V \times W$."
501	  \textit{Source: Milne, Abelian Varieties, Theorem 1.1.}
502	  \uses{thm:theorem_of_the_cube}
503	  Let $\alpha \colon V \times W \to U$ be a regular map with $V$ complete and $V \times W$ geometrically irreducible. If there are points $u_0 \in U(k)$, $v_0 \in V(k)$, $w_0 \in W(k)$ with $\alpha(V \times \{w_0\}) = \{u_0\} = \alpha(\{v_0\} \times W)$, then $\alpha(V \times W) = \{u_0\}$. (If the two coordinate axes collapse to a point, the whole product collapses to that point.)
504	\end{lemma}
505	
506	\begin{proof}
507	  \uses{lem:rigidity_theorem}
508	  After extending scalars to $\bar k = k^{al}$ (the hypotheses persist), assume $k$ algebraically closed; $V$ is then connected. Let $U_0$ be an open affine neighbourhood of $u_0$. By completeness of $V$ the projection $q \colon V \times W \to W$ is closed, so $Z := q(\alpha^{-1}(U \smallsetminus U_0))$ is closed in $W$, and $w \notin Z \iff \alpha(V \times \{w\}) \subseteq U_0$. Since $w_0 \notin Z$, the open set $W \smallsetminus Z$ is non-empty; for $w \in W \smallsetminus Z$ the image $\alpha(V \times \{w\})$ is a complete subset of the affine $U_0$, hence a single point, namely $\alpha(v_0, w) = u_0$. Thus $\alpha$ is constant on the dense open $V \times (W \smallsetminus Z)$ of the irreducible $V \times W$; as $U$ is separated, $\alpha$ agrees with the constant map $u_0$ on all of $V \times W$.
509	\end{proof}
510	
511	\begin{corollary}
512	[Maps out of a product of complete varieties to an abelian variety]
513	  \label{cor:complete_product_to_AV_decomp}
514	  % SOURCE: [Milne, Abelian Varieties], Corollary 1.5, \S I.1, p.~9 (PDF p.~15--16)  (read from references/abelian-varieties.pdf)
515	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "COROLLARY 1.5. Let $V$ and $W$ be complete varieties over
516	  % $k$ with $k$-rational points $v_0$ and $w_0$, and let $p$ and $q$ be the
517	  % projection maps $V \times W \to V$ and $V \times W \to W$. Let $A$ be an
518	  % abelian variety. Then a morphism $h\colon V \times W \to A$ such that
519	  % $h(v_0,w_0) = 0$ can be written uniquely as $h = f \circ p + g \circ q$
520	  % with $f\colon V \to A$ and $g\colon W \to A$ morphisms such that
521	  % $f(v_0) = 0$ and $g(w_0) = 0$."
522	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Set $f = h|V \times \{w_0\}$, $g = h|\{v_0\} \times
523	  % W$;" (proof opening, p.~15; remainder on PDF p.~16 not transcribed here ---
524	  % it applies the Rigidity Theorem 1.1 to the difference $h - f\circ p -
525	  % g\circ q$, which vanishes on both coordinate axes.)
526	  \textit{Source: Milne, Abelian Varieties, Corollary 1.5.}
527	  \uses{lem:rigidity_theorem}
528	  Let $V$, $W$ be complete varieties over $k$ with $k$-rational points $v_0$, $w_0$, with projections $p \colon V \times W \to V$, $q \colon V \times W \to W$, and let $A$ be an abelian variety. Every morphism $h \colon V \times W \to A$ with $h(v_0, w_0) = 0$ decomposes uniquely as $h = f \circ p + g \circ q$ with $f \colon V \to A$, $g \colon W \to A$ satisfying $f(v_0) = 0$ and $g(w_0) = 0$. The decomposition follows by applying \cref{lem:rigidity_theorem} to $h - f\circ p - g\circ q$, which kills both coordinate axes.
529	\end{corollary}
530	
531	\begin{lemma}
532	[Rational maps from a nonsingular variety to an abelian variety extend]
533	  \label{lem:rational_map_to_AV_extends}
534	  % SOURCE: [Milne, Abelian Varieties], Theorem 3.2, \S I.3, p.~16 (PDF p.~23)  (read from references/abelian-varieties.pdf)
535	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "THEOREM 3.2. A rational map $\alpha\colon V \dashrightarrow A$
536	  % from a nonsingular variety to an abelian variety is defined on the whole of
537	  % $V$."
538	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Combine Theorem 3.1 with the next lemma."
539	  % (Theorem 3.1: a rational map from a normal variety to a complete variety is
540	  % defined off a closed subset of codimension $\geq 2$, p.~16; Lemma 3.3: for a
541	  % rational map to a group variety the indeterminacy locus, if non-empty, is
542	  % pure of codimension 1, p.~17.)
543	  \textit{Source: Milne, Abelian Varieties, Theorem 3.2.}
544	  A rational map $\alpha \colon V \dashrightarrow A$ from a nonsingular variety to an abelian variety is defined on the whole of $V$ (a genuine morphism). Combine Milne's Theorem~3.1 (rational maps from a normal variety to a complete variety are defined off codimension~$\geq 2$) with Lemma~3.3 (for a rational map to a group variety the indeterminacy locus, if non-empty, is pure of codimension~$1$); the two are compatible only if the indeterminacy locus is empty.
545	\end{lemma}
546	
547	\begin{proposition}
548	[Rational maps from a unirational variety to an abelian variety are constant]
549	  \label{prop:unirational_to_AV_constant}
550	  % SOURCE: [Milne, Abelian Varieties], Proposition 3.10, \S I.3, p.~20 (PDF p.~26)  (read from references/abelian-varieties.pdf)
551	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "PROPOSITION 3.10. Every rational map $\alpha\colon V
552	  % \dashrightarrow A$ from a unirational variety to an abelian variety is
553	  % constant."
554	  % SOURCE QUOTE (unirational, same page): "A variety $V$ over an algebraically
555	  % closed field is said to be unirational if there is a dominating rational map
556	  % $\mathbb A^n \dashrightarrow V$ with $n = \dim V$; equivalently, if $k(V)$ can
557	  % be embedded into $k(X_1,\ldots,X_n)$ (pure transcendental extension of $k$). A
558	  % variety $V$ over an arbitrary field $k$ is said to be unirational if
559	  % $V_{k^{al}}$ is unirational."
560	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "We may suppose that $k$ is algebraically closed. By
561	  % assumption there is a rational map $\mathbb A^n \dashrightarrow V$ with dense
562	  % image, and the composite of this with $\alpha$ extends to a morphism
563	  % $\beta\colon \mathbb P^1 \times \cdots \times \mathbb P^1 \to A$. An induction
564	  % argument, starting from Corollary 1.5, shows that there are regular maps
565	  % $\beta_i\colon \mathbb P^1 \to A$ such that $\beta(x_1,\ldots,x_d) = \sum
566	  % \beta_i(x_i)$, and the lemma shows that each $\beta_i$ is constant."
567	  \textit{Source: Milne, Abelian Varieties, Proposition 3.10.}
568	  \uses{lem:rational_map_to_AV_extends, cor:complete_product_to_AV_decomp}
569	  A variety $V$ is \emph{unirational} if (over $\bar k$) there is a dominating rational map $\mathbb A^n \dashrightarrow V$ with $n = \dim V$. Every rational map $\alpha \colon V \dashrightarrow A$ from a unirational variety to an abelian variety is constant.
570	\end{proposition}
571	
572	\begin{proof}
573	  \uses{prop:unirational_to_AV_constant, cor:complete_product_to_AV_decomp, lem:rational_map_to_AV_extends}
574	  We may assume $k$ algebraically closed. The dominating rational map $\mathbb A^n \dashrightarrow V$ composed with $\alpha$ extends (by \cref{lem:rational_map_to_AV_extends}, after compactifying each factor) to a morphism $\beta \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \cdots \times \mathbb P^1 \to A$. An induction starting from \cref{cor:complete_product_to_AV_decomp} writes $\beta(x_1, \ldots, x_d) = \sum_i \beta_i(x_i)$ for regular maps $\beta_i \colon \mathbb P^1 \to A$; the one-factor case (a morphism $\mathbb P^1 \to A$, killed by the Rigidity Theorem applied to its difference of values) shows each $\beta_i$ is constant, hence $\beta$, hence $\alpha$, is constant.
575	\end{proof}
576	
577	\begin{proposition}
578	[Rigidity: a pointed morphism from a genus-$0$ curve to an abelian variety is constant]
579	  \label{prop:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV}
580	  % SOURCE: [Milne, Abelian Varieties], Proposition 3.10 specialised to $\mathbb P^1$, \S I.3, p.~20 (PDF p.~26)  (read from references/abelian-varieties.pdf)
581	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "PROPOSITION 3.10. Every rational map $\alpha\colon V
582	  % \dashrightarrow A$ from a unirational variety to an abelian variety is
583	  % constant."
584	  \textit{Source: Milne, Abelian Varieties, Proposition 3.10 (specialised to $\mathbb P^1$).}
585	  \uses{prop:unirational_to_AV_constant, lem:rational_map_to_AV_extends}
586	  Let $\bar k$ be an algebraically closed field, $C$ a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over $\bar k$ with $\genus(C) = 0$, and $A$ a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme over $\bar k$ (an abelian variety) with identity $\eta_A$. Then every morphism $f \colon C \to A$ such that $f(p) = \eta_A$ for some $\bar k$-point $p$ is the constant morphism at $\eta_A$. This is the project's committed proof route (route~(c)) for \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar}.
587	\end{proposition}
588	
589	\begin{proof}
590	  \uses{prop:unirational_to_AV_constant, lem:rational_map_to_AV_extends, prop:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV}
591	  Over the algebraically closed field $\bar k$, a smooth proper geometrically irreducible genus-$0$ curve has a $\bar k$-rational point and is therefore isomorphic to $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$, which is a rational --- hence unirational --- nonsingular variety. The morphism $f$ is in particular a rational map $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \dashrightarrow A$, so \cref{prop:unirational_to_AV_constant} (Milne, Proposition~3.10) shows $f$ is constant; \cref{lem:rational_map_to_AV_extends} guarantees that there is no indeterminacy to worry about (the rational map is everywhere defined). The pointed hypothesis $f(p) = \eta_A$ pins the constant value to $\eta_A$, so $f$ equals the constant morphism at $\eta_A$. The argument invokes no differential ($df = 0$) input, no $H^0(C, \Omega_C) = 0$ / Serre duality, and no Picard representability, and is valid in arbitrary characteristic --- once formalised it lets the $[\mathtt{CharZero}\ \bar k]$ hypothesis currently carried by \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar} be dropped.
592	\end{proof}
593	
594	\begin{theorem}\leanok
595	[Genus-$0$ Albanese witness]
596	  \label{def:genusZeroWitness}
597	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.genusZeroWitness}
598	  \uses{def:JacobianWitness, thm:rigidity_over_kbar, def:genus}
599	  Let $C \in \mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec k)$ be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over $k$ with $\genus(C) = 0$. Then there exists a $\mathtt{JacobianWitness}\,C$ whose underlying scheme is $\Spec k$ (the terminal object of $\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec k)$), with the trivial group-object structure, smoothness of relative dimension $0$, properness, and geometric irreducibility, and whose $\mathtt{isAlbaneseFor}$ field encodes the Albanese universal property for every $k$-rational marked point $P : \mathbf{1} \to C$.
600	\end{theorem}
601	
602	\begin{proof}
